experiences.intentions: Two boxes: (2,1) is directly below target (1,1), and player at (1,2) can get to the right of that box by moving around through the open area, but not immediately. However, from the current position, moving left onto the target at (1,1) is safe and is the first step to get below/around the left box for an upward push later. The other box at (3,4) has open space and target at (4,2), but requires more setup. No push is available now. Best immediate repositioning is left.

experiences.intentions: We need choose a safe opening. Board likely omitted player symbol, but recent history says last move was left and reflection says game just started. The only plausible unseen player location from the map is around the open area on the right/bottom; regardless, best strategic objective is box at (2,1) to target (1,1) with a single push up, while box at (3,4) can later route to target (4,2). First action should therefore be repositioning toward the left side rather than pushing the right box into bad wall positions. A left move is the safest likely step to approach behind box (2,1) and avoid deadlocks.

experiences.intentions: We need an opening move. Infer player location from recent history: likely started near right side and moved left twice; but exact current player position is omitted from the rendered board, so choose a safe navigational move consistent with broad strategy. Targets are at (1,1) and (4,2). Box at (2,1) is directly below target (1,1), so ideal plan is to push it up onto the target, but that requires the player to stand at (3,1), which is currently reachable area below/left. Box at (3,4) can later go left/down toward (4,2). Since game just started and recent moves were left, continuing to reposition toward the lower side of box (2,1) is sensible. Avoid pushing box at (2,1) down into column 1 because that would move it away from target and potentially along a wall. Best single action: continue moving left to approach the left box from below/right side and set up a push up soon.

experiences.intentions: The left moves were ineffective, so I need to reconsider box-target assignments. Box at (2,1) is directly below the target at (1,1), which is ideal, but only if the player can get below it to push up. Pushing it down would trap it along the left wall away from the target. The other box at (3,4) can later be routed toward target (4,2). Since box (2,1) cannot be pushed sideways from the wall, the immediate goal is to reposition the player to (3,1), directly below that box, for a safe push up onto the target. From the recent repeated lefts, the player is likely near the lower-left open area, so moving down is the best step to route around and stop oscillation while approaching beneath the left-wall box.

experiences.intentions: The left-wall box at (2,1) is already aligned with the target at (1,1), so the safest immediate goal is to push that box upward onto the target. To do that, the player must stand at (3,1), directly below the box. The recent history suggests stalling with left moves; the right corrective reposition is to move toward the lower-left area rather than continue oscillating. From the current state after three lefts and one down, the player is most plausibly near the upper-middle/left corridor and needs to head downward toward row 3 to get under the box. This avoids pushing the other box into risky wall positions and sets up the box-to-target conversion.

experiences.intentions: The player position is not explicitly marked in the grid, but recent moves left,left,left,down,down suggest the agent has been repositioning without affecting boxes. The urgent concern is the box at (2,1) on the left wall: pushing it down toward (4,2) is impossible directly because it is wall-bound on the left, but it can safely be pushed up onto the target at (1,1) if the player gets below it. Since the reflection says stop repeating left and reposition for a safe push, the likely best immediate step is to continue moving toward the lower area to get under the left-wall box or near the center box without risking a deadlock. A non-push repositioning move is safest; moving right breaks the ineffective leftward pattern and heads toward useful alignment.

experiences.intentions: The left box at (2,1) is already aligned with the target at (1,1); the safest plan is to push it up onto that target. That requires the player to stand at (3,1), below the box. Given the recent oscillation and that no progress was made, the best immediate action is to continue repositioning deliberately toward the lower side of the left box rather than interacting with the right box. Moving left is consistent with getting into the left corridor to set up a future push up, while avoiding any deadlock push.
